description: Add `gbiv tmux clean` subcommand that closes ROYGBIV-color tmux windows in the project session that have no feature tagged with that color in GBIV.md.

userStories.title: Create clean.rs module scaffold with clean_subcommand()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new `src/commands/tmux/clean.rs` file with a `clean_subcommand()` function and a stub `clean_command()` signature so that subsequent stories have a valid module to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/commands/tmux/clean.rs` exists
`clean_subcommand()` returns a `clap::Command` named `"clean"` with a one-line about string
`clean_command()` function exists with signature `pub fn clean_command() -> Result<(), String>`
`cargo test` passes
userStories.notes: Mirror the structure of new_session.rs. No logic yet — just the skeleton.

userStories.title: Implement guard sequence in clean_command()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `clean_command()` to verify tmux is installed, locate the gbiv project root, and confirm the project tmux session exists before proceeding, so that the command fails fast with actionable errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns `Err("tmux not found. Please install tmux.")` when tmux is not on PATH (mirror new_session.rs guard)
Returns an error when run outside a gbiv project (calls `find_gbiv_root` and handles `None`)
Returns an error suggesting `gbiv tmux new-session` when `tmux has-session -t <folder_name>` fails
`cargo test` passes
userStories.notes: All three guards go in clean_command() in the same order as new_session.rs: tmux check → find root → session check.

userStories.title: Implement core window enumeration, filtering, and kill logic
userStories.description: As a gbiv user, I want `gbiv tmux clean` to automatically close tmux windows whose ROYGBIV color has no tagged feature in GBIV.md, so that stale windows are removed without manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Calls `tmux list-windows -t <session> -F "#{window_name}"` and collects results into a Vec<String>
Parses GBIV.md via `parse_gbiv_md` and collects tagged color values into a HashSet<String>
Only evaluates windows whose name appears in the `COLORS` constant; windows named `main` or any non-ROYGBIV name are silently skipped
A window is orphaned when `COLORS.contains(name) && !active_colors.contains(name)`
Calls `tmux kill-window -t <session>:<name>` for each orphaned window and prints the window name
Prints "Nothing to clean." when no orphaned windows are found
On per-window kill failure, prints a warning, continues remaining windows, and returns non-zero after the loop
`cargo test` passes
userStories.notes: active_colors is built from features where feature.tag.is_some(). Use std::collections::HashSet.

userStories.title: Wire clean subcommand into the tmux command group
userStories.description: As a gbiv user, I want `gbiv tmux clean` to be a recognized subcommand so that I can invoke it from the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub mod clean;` is present in `src/commands/tmux/mod.rs`
`tmux_command()` registers `.subcommand(clean::clean_subcommand())`
`dispatch()` has a `Some(("clean", _)) => clean::clean_command()` arm
`gbiv tmux clean --help` prints usage and exits with status 0
`cargo test` passes
userStories.notes: Only touches src/commands/tmux/mod.rs. Three small edits mirroring how new_session is wired.

userStories.title: Write unit tests for clean_command guards and filtering logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests covering the tmux-not-found guard, the session-not-found guard, and the orphan-filtering logic so that regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Test `tmux not found`: sets PATH to empty dir, calls `clean_command()`, asserts error contains "tmux not found"
Test `session not found`: asserts returned error message mentions `gbiv tmux new-session`

Test orphan filtering (pure logic): given a list of window names and a set of active colors, verify the correct subset is identified as orphaned — `main` and non-ROYGBIV names must never appear in the result
All tests are decorated with `#[serial]` where PATH is mutated
`cargo test` passes
userStories.notes: Extract the filtering predicate into a testable pure function to avoid requiring a live tmux process.
